content.answer:

   the shop name issue? Where did we land on that

discussed with kr team on the naming, no conclusions, need to wait kr team
think it through to design the naming rules.

we decided to request krsip first, with a very low effort script to mass
change shop names. the naming is not a blocker for the project.

content.question:
as long as we do not use the official brand name to sell, is this something
the brands would be fine with and accept to be on SIP?

content.answer:
--> For SIP name, I'd suggest to use the current P shop's name but just
take out the word "official" and keep the brand name. So for brand shop
example, [torridenofficial.os] will be [torriden.th]. Since sellers will
need to consent in one form or the other, they will want their names in it,
but not project the SIP shop as the flagship and just more sensitive with
the word "official".

Slide 6 Haenle Suh <haenle.suh@shopee.com>

   -

   Is there a reason why JPCB non-ads TR% ~3% lower than KRCB?

->(Please refer to below table pointer #1) When looking at L3M(‘25 Aug-Oct
PnL) actuals, the non-ads TR% gap mainly comes from 3 factors; commission
TR%, mkt pck TR% and handling fee TR%;

Commission TR% for JP is lower vs. KR due to ~10%p lower G2N(e.g for Oct JP
G2N 67% vs KR G2N 76%)  and mkt pck TR% lower due to not having enough big
sellers to buy year long CBP(e.g. KR UST Beauty brands year long CBP
enrollment in all markets)

 [image: image.png]

Slide 8 Haenle Suh <haenle.suh@shopee.com>

   -

   What is the issue regarding G2N?

-> No issue as the commission TR% gap(0.18mil out of 0.55mil PC2 gap as of
11/20 previous sharing) was based on “wrong” G2N data as Reg BI didn’t have
CB BU split. Thus now fixed with KRJP specific G2N data, we do not see much
gap in commission TR% nor significant drop in the G2N

-> For your reference, as of 11/26, KRJP PC2 & SLS PnL is above target with
RR 116%(Total 2.45mil - PC2 missing target by 0.13mil but SLS PnL making up
with surplus of 0.5mil) and majority of the PC2 gap coming from KR ads TR%
gap (please refer to below table blue box)

[image: image.png]


Slide 14 – 16 Yuna Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com> Junho Kim
<junho.kim@shopee.com>

   -

   As discussed please go ahead and I suspect Cosrx and Somebymi fall under
   D as compared to A

→ Cosrx is type A they have local OS (MY)
<https://shopee.com.my/cosrx.my?categoryId=100630&entryPoint=ShopByPDP&itemId=29000297333&upstream

content.question:
--> For SIP name, I'd suggest to use the current P shop's name but just
take out the word "official" and keep the brand name. So for brand shop
example, [torridenofficial.os] will be [torriden.th]. Since sellers will
need to consent in one form or the other, they will want their names in it,
but not project the SIP shop as the flagship and just more sensitive with
the word "official".



Ok, we can try this if we feel it has the highest chance of getting seller
approval.



I think we may need to give a few use case of other brands that we have
provided this service for, to allay the fears that we mis-manage the brand.



Slide 6 Haenle Suh <haenle.suh@shopee.com>

   - Is there a reason why JPCB non-ads TR% ~3% lower than KRCB?

->(Please refer to below table pointer #1) When looking at L3M(‘25 Aug-Oct
PnL) actuals, the non-ads TR% gap mainly comes from 3 factors; commission
TR%, mkt pck TR% and handling fee TR%;

Commission TR% for JP is lower vs. KR due to ~10%p lower G2N(e.g for Oct JP
G2N 67% vs KR G2N 76%)  and mkt pck TR% lower due to not having enough big
sellers to buy year long CBP(e.g. KR UST Beauty brands year long CBP
enrollment in all markets)



So for commissions/service fee/handling fee, it is the same for KR vs JP?
Is there any difference between Mall/Non-Mall?
Did we make JP sellers mandatory FSS as well?

Slide 14 – 16 Yuna Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com> Junho Kim
<junho.kim@shopee.com>

   - As discussed please go ahead and I suspect Cosrx and Somebymi fall
   under D as compared to A

→ Cosrx is type A they have local OS (MY)
<https://shopee.com.my/cosrx.my?categoryId=100630&entryPoint=ShopByPDP&itemId=29000297333&upstream=search>
run by a local vendor glampick. But the assortment still short of TTS
(different vendor storeNgo). But still need to close gap of assortment. So
even if they fall under A, we still treat them as D and arranged T2T
meetings with both brands in the week of 8Dec



I see. I think the preference was always StoreNgo compared to Gampick
